summary: Phase 3.6 refactor verified end-to-end. (a) DriveStatusContext lifts /drive/status to a single fetch per page-load — confirmed exactly ONE GET /api/drive/status across the entire post-login session: /dashboard → /editor → user dropdown → /dashboard → /post/{id}. Disconnect via AlertDialog flips the menu (Drive connected → Connect Google Drive) and removes the editor hint WITHOUT manual reload; the context's setConnected(false) drives the UI immediately. (b) asyncio.to_thread() refactor: 5 concurrent /api/upload requests via httpx.AsyncClient + asyncio.gather all returned 200 with distinct paths (12.78s wall — confirms the event loop is no longer blocked on storage put_object). All 44 existing pytest backend tests still pass + 1 new concurrency test = 45/45.
critical_code_review_comments: DriveStatusContext.jsx is clean: useMemo on the context value prevents needless consumer re-renders; refresh() short-circuits when user is null (so logout does NOT trigger a wasted /drive/status call — verified).
useDriveStatus.js correctly re-exports from the context for backward compatibility — no callers needed editing beyond removing the `user` arg.
Provider order in App.js is correct: DriveStatusProvider sits INSIDE AuthProvider (it depends on useAuth) and wraps both AppShell + Toaster.

asyncio.to_thread wrappers in server.py are applied at every blocking site: creds.refresh, googleapiclient.discovery.build, flow.fetch_token, files().list/create/update().execute() via inner def closures, and storage put_object/get_object — pattern is consistent and correct.

Minor (dev-only) observation: React.StrictMode in index.js causes a 2x effect invocation on hard reload in development → GET /api/drive/status fires twice on full page-reload (e.g., after page.goto). This is expected dev-mode behavior and would be ONE fetch in production build. Not a regression.
Pre-existing item still standing: html.escape() should be applied to post.title in the Drive export HTML template (server.py) — future hardening, unrelated to this phase.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_async_concurrency.py (NEW — verifies asyncio.to_thread releases the event loop under 5 concurrent uploads)
seed_data_creation: Inserted fake drive_credentials doc for demo user_id to simulate connected state (twice — once for the disconnect flow, once for the publish-with-drive E2E). Cleaned up at end of testing. Created and removed 1 TEST_RefactorE2E_NotConnected and 1 TEST_RefactorE2E_Connected post during e2e. DB returned to baseline state (only original demo posts).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3.6 confirmed: single /drive/status fetch per page-load lifecycle works; asyncio.to_thread releases event loop. Re-use /app/backend/tests/test_async_concurrency.py for any future regression on /api/upload concurrency. To re-test the connected-state UI: insert into db.drive_credentials for demo user_id=user_ee063d86f797 (see iteration_5 seed schema). After hard page reload (page.goto) you'll see 2 /drive/status fetches due to React.StrictMode in dev — count fetches across SOFT navigations (clicking Link/Navlink) only, where the count should be 1 per session lifecycle. All Navbar/Editor/PostView selectors documented in iteration_5 still valid.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found. The refactor passed every acceptance criterion: single fetch per page-load, immediate UI flip after disconnect, hint visibility matches connection state, no /drive/status fetch after logout, all backend tests green, concurrent uploads non-blocking with distinct outputs.
